Module 6:
Get the Deal
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Welcome to the Last Module: G
It’s time to Get the Deal.
But first...
Let’s take a look at how we got here...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Where Everyone Starts ...
You got a product, idea or company you wanted to sell. Next step?
You got in front of people to tell them about it.
Probably:
You tried some “sales” techniques (like the books tell you to do.)
“Well, Mr. Jones, I understand how you feel. I felt that way myself when I first encountered the red. But
I found after time it really grew on me and I learned to love it.”
“So you want it in red. If I can get it to you in red, would you take it today?”
But these aren’t authentic, genuine or connected to the real you.
Or you maybe you tried Feel - Felt - Found?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
The Real You. Yes ... that’s it!
“Just be yourself.”
So you went the natural route.
Why not?
There’s REAL value in what you have to offer.
But ... let’s admit it. We bored people with our natural pitch.
You recognized your pitch was not structured.
And - perhaps you made it seem a little more complicated than it really is...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Time to Build Structure
That’s solid thinking right there.
“The better I pitch, the higher my close-ratio will be.”
You sought out better ways to present, persuade and promote.
You were introduced to Pitch Anything and the S.T.R.O.N.G. Method.

www.pitchanything.com

www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
S.T.R.O.N.G. Pitch Model
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Money Has Yet to Transact
It’s one thing to get a purchase and sale agreement.
It’s another thing to get the money transferred to your account.
“There’s many a slip between the cup and the lip.”
That’s why we need to know how to Get the Deal.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
3 Common Scenarios
1. The customer/buyer/partner loves your deal.
If you have a PROCESS, put them in it immediately.
2. The buyer will have one last attempt at gaining a concession.
This is so common, you should expect it. The way to deal with it? Commit strongly to your response.
If you are going to concede, do it with high status,
“Whatever, if 5% means that much to you, who cares. We focus on the doing things right, not grinding
deals to the bone.”
or:
“Uh, NO. That’s not going to happen.”
3. There are still small terms to negotiate.
Common in investments. Closing date, deposit, bad boy carveouts, etc.
Work through them.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
3 Main Principles
There are three main principles to consider:
1. The Importance of Autonomy
“We have the best deal, the strongest product, best service, but of course I’m bias. Together, we’ve
talked about the value of the offer, but I can’t tell you what to do- you need to decide for yourself.”
2. The Destructive Force of Neediness
3. The Importance of Commitment
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Autonomy
Gnawing, nagging, clawing or crowding your buyer.
He’s going to lose a sense of autonomy.
If you limit your buyer’s self-determination, you are courting disaster.
The deal is going to blow up.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Neediness
A primary trigger of fear in the brain of the buyer.
I choose that word carefully. I said the brain …
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Social Tools
We have talked about many social tools in Pitch Mastery.
Eradicating neediness.
Time Constraint
Intrigue
Tension
Moral Authority
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
But You Have To Commit
Time constraint is only meaningful when you commit to it.
You have to be willing to walk away.
You are willing to stop the deal if they keep pushing for a discount.
You only take on 10 new customers a year. That’s it.
If you say these things to drive the deal - you also have to fully commit.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
You Could Say It’s Mythic
Like a hero from an old Western.
He pulls aside the young gunhand, looks him in the eye:
“Say what you mean, and mean what you say.”
It’s hardcoded in us to admire values, integrity, principled behavior.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
What Compelling Characters Are Made Of
Tough situations. Difficult calls.
Doing the right thing even when it’s not easy.
Keeping your word. Doing what you say even when it’s not convenient.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Consequences
This means you WILL lose deals.
Sometimes you tell someone, “you have to commit by Friday.” And then ... ooops ... they don’t commit
by the deadline.
So on Monday, have to you tell him, “You didn’t commit. You’re out. I’m going with another buyer.”
You’ll lose that guy.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 18 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
You Will Get More Business
For every one of those guys you lose, you’ll win another FIVE.
Why?
The basic nature of mankind.
We want what we cannot have.
We chase that which moves away from us.
We only value the things we pay for.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 18 --

Module 6:
Get the Deal
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 18 --
